content.text:
Based on my detailed examination of the molecule from multiple angles, I can now identify the neighbour atoms of C:11.

Looking at the zoomed view of atom C:11 (index 11, element C), I can clearly see the following covalent bonds:

**Neighbour atoms of C:11:**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|--------------|---------|
| 10 | C |
| 12 | C |
| 14 | N |
| 25 | C |

**Summary:** Atom C:11 (Carbon) has 4 neighbour atoms:
- **C:10** (Carbon) - to the right
- **C:12** (Carbon) - to the upper left
- **N:14** (Nitrogen) - above (shown in blue)
- **C:25** (Carbon) - to the lower left

**geomconf_index:** 417

The carbon atom C:11 forms four covalent bonds (as expected for carbon), connecting to three other carbon atoms and one nitrogen atom, making it a quaternary carbon center in this molecular structure.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 11, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
